title: An additive Hamiltonian plus Landauer's Principle yields quantum theory
abstract: It is shown that no-signalling, a quantum of action, unitarity, detailed balance, Bell's theorem, the Hilbert-space representation of physical states and the Born rule all follow from the assumption of an additive Hamiltonian together with Landauer's principle. Common statements of the classical limit of quantum theory, as well as common assumptions made by interpretations of quantum theory, contradict additivity, Landauer's principle, or both.
